title: Empty are the Mother's Arms.
content:
Ah, empty are the mother's arms
        Which clasp a vanished form;
    A darling spared from life's alarms,
        And safe from earthly storm.

    In absent reverie, she hears
        That voice, nor can forget;
    The fond illusion disappears,--
        Her arms are empty, yet.
meaning:
Alfred Castner King's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Empty are the Mother's Arms."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
